storyline:
NuScale Power (SMR) builds small modular reactors. It ran up through mid-2024 on the nuclear-revival trade — AI datacenters scrambling for clean baseload power — driven by a discrete, datable catalyst: the ADVANCE Act (Accelerating Deployment of Versatile, Advanced Nuclear for Clean Energy Act), a bipartisan bill streamlining NRC licensing and fees for small modular reactors. The solution catches SMR early (Apr-26, conviction 7, the bill advancing), rides the runup - conviction fading through the legislative lull, then resetting to 7 on the Jun-18 Senate passage - and hard-exits Jul-12 the moment the President signs the bill (Jul-9), the catalyst resolved. Classic buy-the-rumor / sell-the-news: SMR peaked ~Jul-15 then fell ~44%, and exiting at the signing dodged the crash (pick +102%, portfolio +116%, maxDD -16%). The cleanest arc in the set - one named catalyst driving both the entry and the exit.
This gem’s event-agent (ev1) — catalyst: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing bill advances in Congress. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2024-07-12 — President signed ADVANCE Act into law 7/9; the discrete legislative catalyst has fully resolved.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • defensive · GLD — catalyst: always-on defensive (GLD) floor agent. Exit: still live (catalyst active).

seeds.title: NuScale Power (SMR) rides the nuclear renaissance as the ADVANCE Act advances and AI data centers scramble for power
seeds.snippet: NuScale Power (SMR), the only US small-modular-reactor developer with an NRC-approved design, is climbing as the ADVANCE Act - a bipartisan bill that streamlines NRC licensing and fees for advanced and small modular reactors - moves through Congress, while AI data centers scramble for clean baseload power. As the purest SMR pure-play, SMR is the most direct way to own the licensing overhaul, still under-owned ahead of the bill's passage and signing.

seeds.title: The ADVANCE Act clears the Senate; the SMR-licensing overhaul heads to the President's desk
seeds.snippet: The Senate passed the ADVANCE Act on June 18, sending the nuclear-licensing overhaul - which streamlines NRC review and fees for small modular reactors - to the President for signature. NuScale Power (SMR), the leading US SMR pure-play, extends its run as the discrete, datable legislative catalyst approaches its resolution at signing.

seeds.title: President signs the ADVANCE Act into law; NuScale (SMR) tops out on the buy-the-rumor, sell-the-news
seeds.snippet: The President signed the ADVANCE Act into law on July 9, enacting streamlined NRC licensing for small modular reactors. With the discrete legislative catalyst now RESOLVED, NuScale Power (SMR) - which had run up sharply into the signing - topped out around July 12 as the buy-the-rumor gave way to sell-the-news, and the stock began a steep decline. The driving catalyst is done.

SMR.thesis: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing bill advances in Congress
SMR.exit_case: none — ADVANCE Act had not yet been signed into law as of this date; still moving through Congress
SMR.assessment: Entry week. ADVANCE Act is a bipartisan bill streamlining NRC licensing for advanced reactors, still advancing through Congress (not yet passed Senate/House or signed by President as of 4/26/24). SMR (NuScale) is the only NRC-design-approved US SMR developer and the cleanest pure-play, benefiting from both the bill's momentum and AI-driven power demand tailwind. Concrete next steps remain ahead (further congressional votes, eventual signing), so this is early-stage, not yet mainstream-saturated or resolved.
SMR.exit_advice: Hold; exit only when Act is signed into law (priced) or licensing reform is reversed/stalled indefinitely

SMR.thesis: ADVANCE Act nuclear licensing bill advances in Congress
SMR.exit_case: President signed ADVANCE Act into law 7/9; the discrete legislative catalyst has fully resolved.
SMR.assessment: The ADVANCE Act catalyst has completed its arc: Senate passage (6/18) -> presidential signature (7/9), confirmed by energy.gov and multiple financial outlets. This is exactly the acute, datable resolution the framework requires exiting on - the driving condition (bill pending in Congress) no longer exists; it is now law. Fool.com's coverage explicitly frames this as the legislation event driving today's pop, and the energy.gov headline itself flags 'buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news' - textbook priced-in/resolved signal. No concrete next legislative milestone remains for this specific catalyst (NRC rulemaking implementation is a separate, diffuse regulatory process, not a scheduled datable event this thesis was built on). Structurally, broader nuclear/SMR/AI-power-demand themes may continue, but the SPECIFIC catalyst entered on - the bill advancing/passing/signing - is now fully resolved and public. Per rules, catalyst_resolved=true mandates thesis_live=false regardless of continued share strength.
SMR.exit_advice: Exit. Bill signed into law 7/9 - catalyst fully resolved and public; headline itself notes 'buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news' dynamic on SMR. Any further upside is now broader AI-power-demand/uranium theme, not this specific bill catalyst. Close position.
